channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The Simplest Way I Found to Build Drag and Drop in React and Next.js
item.description: My Experience Using dnd-kit in React and Next.js For a long time, I avoided building drag-and-drop features in frontend projects 😅 Not because I did not need them. Mostly because drag and drop...

item.title: Meet 'Devto-Blogger': The Hermes Agent Skill That Automatically Writes Your Technical Blog Posts
item.description: If you are an open-source maintainer, developer advocate, or builder, you know the cycle: you build an amazing tool, but writing the launch blog post, documentation, or tutorial takes hours. For...

item.title: Your Git Log Is a Legal Document
item.description: In 2024, Orca Security sued Wiz and demanded their full git version control history. Orca wanted to see "when features and functions were added, modified, or altered, including through engineers'...

item.title: Aweskill: Let Your AI Agent Manage skill itself
item.description: Let Your AI Agent Manage skill itself Most developer tools still assume the human is the operator. You read the documentation. You install the CLI. You decide where files should go. You copy...

item.title: I built a cryptographic audit receipt for Claude Mythos (and any AI model) — here's how it works
item.description: Anthropic's Mythos model can autonomously find zero-day vulnerabilities. Their CVD disclosure process uses manual SHA-3-512 hash commitments to prove findings existed. I built something that...

item.title: The Dead Economy Theory

item.title: Secure Your Microservices: Meet Halimun, the High-Performance Encrypted Proxy
item.description: Meet Halimun Proxy a high-performance, ultra-low latency proxy tunnel system built from the ground up in Rust. Why Rust? By leveraging Rust, Halimun achieves extreme efficiency. Using the Axum web...

item.title: From Skeleton to Production: Building HR Goal Tracking Portal with GitHub Copilot
item.description: This is a submission for the GitHub Finish-Up-A-Thon Challenge What I Built I built the Atomberg Goal Setting & Tracking Portal — a full-featured, role-based HR performance management system for...

item.title: The GSoC Arc: How I Almost Didn't Show Up to My Own Story

item.title: The Vatican’s Man Inside Anthropic
item.description: Pope Leo XIV may not be able to disarm AI, but he’s got the attention of the industry.

item.title: Trump’s mass deportations are only possible with racial profiling
item.description: Border security czar Tom Homan keeps threatening to "flood" New York City with ICE agents. But a new investigation shows that ICE has been quietly ramping up arrests in the New York area already -...

item.title: At $549, Lenovo’s Legion Go S gaming handheld is suddenly a good deal
item.description: This week, the Steam Deck OLED with 512GB of storage went from $549 to $789, putting it even further out of reach for those who were considering getting one as they came back in stock after months...

item.title: You can buy two of Anker’s Qi2 wireless chargers for under $25
item.description: If you’re looking for a fast iPhone or AirPods charger that’s easy to toss into your purse, backpack, or carry-on, Anker’s Zolo Magnetic Wireless Charger is a smart pick. It’s tiny and comes with...

item.title: Attackers Use LLM Agent for Post-Exploitation After Marimo CVE-2026-39987 Exploit
item.description: An unknown threat actor has been observed using a large language model (LLM) agent to conduct post-compromise actions after obtaining initial access following the exploitation of a...

item.title: Today is the last day to apply to speak at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.description: Submit your session topic before today ends for a chance to speak at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Apply now to share your insight and help shape the conversations defining the tech industry.

item.title: Final 24 hours to save up to $410 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket
item.description: You now have until tonight at 11:59 p.m. PT to lock in Early Bird savings of up to $410 for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 before prices increase. Join 10,000+ tech leaders in October for one of the most...

item.title: How Ferrari bungled the design of its first EV
item.description: For nearly 80 years, Ferrari occupied a unique cultural space where its cars were aspirational, even for people who resented those who could afford them. The price, the exclusivity, and the...

item.title: What would you be willing to put in your body?
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in...

item.title: Kiwibit’s AI-powered bird feeder is my new backyard buddy
item.description: If you're looking for a fun way to connect with nature while collecting bird species on an app like Pokémon, give this smart feeder a try.

item.title: Backrooms is at the forefront of horror’s YouTube wave
item.description: Though YouTube has always been a place where up-and-coming artists could be discovered and make it big, in recent years the platform has become a launching pad for some of Hollywood's most...

item.title: Acer’s answer to the MacBook Neo is a $699 laptop with Intel chips and 8GB of RAM
item.description: Acer is aiming to take on the MacBook Neo more directly with a new Swift Air 14, a new 14-inch laptop that starts at $699. The laptop will use Intel's lower-cost Core Series 3 "Wildcat Lake" chips...

item.title: Trump Mobile’s website still says the T1 Phone is ‘American-made’
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. As the phone's supposed launch continues, we...

item.title: Jony Ive’s funky Ferrari
item.description: Most people will never own, drive, or even sit inside a Ferrari Luce. (If you can, or do… hit us up.) There's still no question that Ferrari's first electric vehicle is one of the most...

item.title: The Download: unlocking lithium and controlling Ebola
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How a new extraction process could unlock the world’s...

item.title: This chip startup just raised $135M on a bet that AI’s biggest bottleneck isn’t compute — it’s memory
item.description: South Korean chip startup XCENA is betting that AI's real bottleneck is not compute, but memory.

item.title: Boston Children’s uses AI to unlock new diagnoses
item.description: Boston Children’s Hospital uses OpenAI technology to improve patient care, reduce operational burden, and help diagnose more than 40 rare disease cases.

item.title: Check out real-life AI prototypes from the Futures Lab.
item.description: University of Waterloo students develop AI prototypes like sign language tutors to reshape the future of education and work.

item.title: Hundreds of prolific Wikipedia editors are threatening to go on strike
item.description: Wikipedia is one of the last bastions of trust on the internet. But last week, volunteer editors and contributors were alarmed to hear that a small but important team of engineers at the nonprofit...

item.title: New Russian-Linked GREYVIBE Targets Ukraine with AI-Powered Cyberattacks
item.description: A previously undocumented threat actor dubbed GREYVIBE has been attributed to ongoing and persistent attacks targeting Ukraine and Ukraine-related entities since at least August 2025. GREYVIBE,...
